goal: Structural reference fixture: documents expected Orchestrator behavior when a user requests planning or implementation and no plan_path or active plan exists. Orchestrator must dispatch Planner as an entry-point delegate (not a phase executor), receive the returned plan_path, and route it into the existing Planning Gate and PLAN_REVIEW trigger evaluation without treating it as implicit implementation approval.

inputs.label: no-plan-path-user-requests-implementation
inputs.description: User asks Orchestrator to implement a new feature. No plan_path or active plan artifact exists. Orchestrator must delegate to Planner for initial plan creation before any implementation can proceed.

input.query: Add rate limiting to all API endpoints
input.context: No existing plan_path. No active in-memory plan. User is asking for a new capability — Orchestrator has no plan artifact to execute against.

inputs.label: no-plan-path-user-requests-planning
inputs.description: User explicitly asks Orchestrator to plan a task. No plan_path exists. Orchestrator dispatches Planner, waits for plan_path handoff, then enters PLAN_REVIEW trigger evaluation.

input.query: Plan a migration of the user authentication system to OAuth2
input.context: No existing plan_path. User is asking to plan, not to execute. Orchestrator delegates plan authorship to Planner.

inputs.label: existing-plan-path-no-dispatch
inputs.description: Orchestrator receives a request with an existing plan_path already provided. The Initial Planner Dispatch Gate must not trigger — Orchestrator continues directly through the Planning Gate and PLAN_REVIEW evaluation.

input.context: plan_path already exists — Orchestrator should not re-dispatch Planner.

expected.model_resolution_note: Before Planner returns a plan artifact, no complexity_tier exists. Orchestrator must use the target role's top-level primary model per the Universal Model Resolution Rule — never omit model because tier context is missing.
expected.plan_path_contract: Planner returns plan_path as a handoff artifact. Orchestrator treats it as a reviewable input entering the Planning Gate and PLAN_REVIEW trigger evaluation, identical to any other plan artifact. It is not implementation approval.
assertions: Orchestrator has an explicit Initial Planner Dispatch Gate that activates only when no plan_path or active plan exists and the user requests planning or implementation.
The gate dispatches Planner as an entry-point delegate using the Universal Model Resolution Rule with the top-level primary model because no complexity_tier exists yet.
Planner's returned plan_path enters the Planning Gate and PLAN_REVIEW trigger evaluation — it does not bypass user approval or review gates.
Planner does not become a phase executor; it remains the plan-authorship agent whose output Orchestrator coordinates.
When a plan_path is already available, the Initial Planner Dispatch Gate does not trigger.
